message:
feat(harness,tui): expand e2e test harness and TUI rendering capabilities

Major expansion of the ffs-harness e2e test infrastructure and TUI
rendering layer:

ffs-harness/src/e2e.rs (+1023 lines):
- Add comprehensive e2e test scenarios covering filesystem operations,
  MVCC snapshot visibility, block device I/O, and recovery paths
- Include deterministic replay fixtures with structured log output

ffs-harness/src/main.rs (+52 lines):
- Extend harness CLI with new subcommands for running e2e matrix
  and individual packet test scenarios

ffs-tui/src/lib.rs (+250 lines):
- Expand TUI rendering with new view modes and display components
- Add support for additional status panels and diagnostic overlays

Also updates beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-19k","title":"MVCC: Implement MVCC-aware block device wrapper (read snapshot -> version or base)","description":"Goal: make block reads/writes snapshot-aware.

Deliverables:
- Define MVCCBlockDevice (or similar) that wraps an underlying BlockDevice plus a version store.
- Read path: read_visible(block, snapshot) else fall back to base device read_block().
- Write path: stage_write(block, bytes) into the current transaction.
- Ensure block buffers are block_size aligned and validated.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests demonstrate snapshot visibility with base+versioned reads.
- No global locks on read path; concurrency model documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:33.557055409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:13:45.798997520Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:13:45.798977844Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19k","depends_on_id":"bd-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:23:53.105838366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-19k","depends_on_id":"bd-2fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:23:53.024630399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a9","title":"ext4: Implement feature flag decoding + validation policy (compat/ro_compat/incompat)","description":"Goal: represent ext4 feature flags in a way that is (1) easy to validate, (2) easy to print for UX, and (3) safe against unknown bits.

Deliverables:
- Define bitflags (or equivalent) for compat/ro_compat/incompat.
- Implement decode helpers that produce:
  - required bits missing?
  - explicitly rejected bits present?
  - unknown incompatible bits present?
- v1 mount policy:
  - require FILETYPE + EXTENTS
  - block sizes limited to 1K/2K/4K
  - reject compression/encrypt/casefold/inline-data, etc.

Acceptance:
- validate_v1() returns ParseError with stable field+reason.
- Tests cover: required bits missing, unknown incompat bit, known rejected bit, allowed-but-nonrequired bits.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:56.882710351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:10:51.691798163Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:10:51.691780019Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","ondisk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a9","depends_on_id":"bd-2cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.539865706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1b7","title":"Implement btrfs COW B-tree mutation (insert/delete/split/merge)","description":"# Implement btrfs COW B-tree mutation (insert/delete/split/merge)

## GOAL
Implement the core btrfs COW B-tree mutation operations required for write path.

## BACKGROUND
btrfs uses COW semantics for all tree modifications:
- Never modify existing nodes in place
- Create new nodes with modifications
- Update parent pointers up to root
- Old root becomes part of transaction log

This is naturally compatible with MVCC version chains.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Core Operations
```rust
pub trait BtrfsBTree {
    /// Insert key-value pair, returns new root
    fn insert(&self, key: BtrfsKey, item: &[u8], cx: &Cx) -> Result<BlockNumber>;
    
    /// Delete key, returns new root (or same if not found)
    fn delete(&self, key: &BtrfsKey, cx: &Cx) -> Result<BlockNumber>;
    
    /// Update existing key with new value
    fn update(&self, key: &BtrfsKey, item: &[u8], cx: &Cx) -> Result<BlockNumber>;
    
    /// Range query
    fn range(&self, start: &BtrfsKey, end: &BtrfsKey, cx: &Cx) 
        -> Result<Vec<(BtrfsKey, Vec<u8>)>>;
}
```

### 2. COW Mechanics
```rust
/// Clone-on-write a node with modification
fn cow_node(
    original: BlockNumber,
    modification: NodeMod,
    allocator: &dyn BtrfsAllocator,
    cx: &Cx,
) -> Result<BlockNumber> {
    // 1. Read original node
    // 2. Apply modification (insert/delete/update item)
    // 3. Allocate new block
    // 4. Write modified node to new block
    // 5. Return new block number
}
```

### 3. Split/Merge
- Split when node is full (> max items)
- Merge when node is underfull (< min items)
- Rebalance siblings before merge

### 4. Path Walking
- Track path from root to leaf for COW propagation
- Each level gets new block on modification
- Efficient path reuse for sequential inserts

## TESTS
1. Unit: Insert into empty tree
2. Unit: Insert causing split
3. Unit: Delete causing merge
4. Unit: COW produces new root
5. Integration: 1000 random inserts/deletes, tree invariants hold
6. Property: Random operations maintain sorted order

## LOGGING
- Tree modification (trace): operation, key, old root, new root
- Split/merge events (debug): node level, item counts
- Allocation (trace): new block numbers

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All operations preserve B-tree invariants
2. COW produces independent tree versions
3. No in-place modification of shared blocks
4. Performance: O(log N) for all operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:23.658366987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:18.160085044Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:18.160001627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btree","btrfs","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1b7","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:08.481147860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1b7","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:39.683503676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1b7","depends_on_id":"bd-375.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:08.388968210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":48,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BTRFS COW SEMANTICS: btrfs B-tree mutation is inherently COW—every modification creates a new node and updates the parent's pointer. This propagates up to the root, which gets a new generation number. This is fundamentally different from ext4 extent trees (in-place mutation + journal). The COW chain must be atomic: either all nodes in the chain are updated, or none are.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:59Z"},{"id":59,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: added in-memory COW btrfs mutation core in crates/ffs-btrfs (BtrfsBTree trait + InMemoryCowBtrfsTree with insert/update/delete/range, split logic, root COW propagation, invariant validator, and deterministic random mutation test). Added tracing instrumentation for insert/split/delete paths. Full required gates pass (fmt/check/clippy/test workspace). Remaining gap before closure: optimize delete path from rebuild-based fallback to strict O(log N) merge/rebalance semantics and integrate allocator-backed block lifecycle for production write path wiring.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:36Z"},{"id":60,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation note: full workspace gates currently blocked by concurrent non-bd-1b7 compile errors in crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs (E0502 at line ~6190, E0133 at line ~6412) and warning in crates/ffs-mvcc/src/sharded.rs. Package-local gates for ffs-btrfs pass (fmt, test, clippy). Awaiting coordination on cross-bead workspace breakage before closure.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:02:37Z"},{"id":61,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Pausing active work to pick next high-impact bead per bv robot triage. Current implementation is in place; remaining closure blocker is full-workspace gate failure from concurrent non-bd-1b7 edits (ffs-core/ffs-mvcc).","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:04:14Z"},{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed from bv robot-next and started closure triage.

Current state from code audit (crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs):
- Delete path is already strict O(log N): delete_from + rebalance_child + rotate_from_left/right + merge_adjacent_nodes (no tree rebuild fallback in current implementation).
- Primary remaining closure gap is allocator-backed block lifecycle integration:
  - alloc_node currently only bumps in-memory next_block and inserts cloned nodes into nodes map.
  - No corresponding allocator-backed free path for superseded COW nodes.

Coordination status:
- Reservation conflict on crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs (holder: BrightForge, reservation id 21, expires ~09:32Z).
- Sent coordination message in Agent Mail thread bd-1b7; will proceed with allocator lifecycle implementation once reservation is released/handed off.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:04Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed allocator-backed COW node lifecycle integration in crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs.

Implemented:
- Added `BtrfsAllocator` lifecycle interface and default `InMemoryBtrfsAllocator`.
- `InMemoryCowBtrfsTree` now supports custom allocator injection (`with_allocator`) and records deferred frees.
- Wired allocation through allocator (`alloc_node`) and deferred-free tracking (`retire_node`).
- Added deferred-free lifecycle hooks through mutation flow:
  - `insert_into` retires replaced node IDs.
  - `delete_from` retires replaced leaf/internal nodes.
  - `rebalance_child` retires replaced/merged sibling nodes.
  - `normalize_root_after_delete` retires collapsed root nodes.
- Added regression test: `cow_mutations_record_deferred_node_frees`.

Validation:
- cargo fmt --check PASS
- cargo check --all-targets PASS
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS
- cargo test --workspace PASS

Closure note:
- Prior bead note cited delete-path fallback as a remaining concern; current implementation already uses O(log N) rebalance/merge, and this change closes the allocator lifecycle gap.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:18Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1bx","title":"Implement degradation level display in TUI dashboard","description":"# Implement degradation level display in TUI dashboard

## GOAL
Add degradation level and pressure indicators to the TUI dashboard so operators can monitor system health.

## BACKGROUND
The graceful degradation system (bd-3tz) produces state that must be visible:
- Current degradation level (0-4)
- CPU/memory/IO pressure gauges
- Recent degradation events

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Extended DashboardSnapshot
```rust
pub struct DashboardSnapshot {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    // Degradation state
    pub degradation_level: DegradationLevel,
    pub cpu_pressure: f64,      // 0.0 - 1.0
    pub memory_pressure: f64,   // 0.0 - 1.0
    pub io_queue_depth: usize,
    pub degradation_events: Vec<DegradationEvent>,
}
```

### 2. New Panel: System Health
```
┌─ System Health ─────────────────┐
│ Degradation: NORMAL (Level 0)   │
│                                 │
│ CPU:    [████████░░] 78%        │
│ Memory: [██████░░░░] 62%        │
│ I/O:    [███░░░░░░░] 34%        │
│                                 │
│ Recent events:                  │
│   12:34:05 Level 0 → 1 (CPU)    │
│   12:35:22 Level 1 → 0          │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3. Color Coding
- Level 0 (Normal): Green
- Level 1 (Background paused): Yellow
- Level 2 (Cache reduced): Orange
- Level 3 (Writes throttled): Red
- Level 4 (Read-only): Blinking Red

### 4. Gauge Widgets
```rust
fn render_pressure_gauge(value: f64, width: u16) -> String {
    let filled = (value * width as f64) as u16;
    let bar = "█".repeat(filled as usize) + &"░".repeat((width - filled) as usize);
    format!("[{}] {:>3}%", bar, (value * 100.0) as u8)
}
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Gauge rendering at various values
2. Unit: Color coding for each level
3. Integration: Dashboard updates on level change
4. Visual: Screenshot test for layout

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Degradation level always visible
2. Pressure gauges update in real-time
3. Event history shows last 10 events
4.  {"id":"bd-1c4y","title":"EPIC: S3-FIFO for ARC Buffer/Cache Eviction (Graveyard Entry 15.1)","description":"# EPIC: S3-FIFO for ARC Buffer/Cache Eviction (Graveyard Entry 15.1)

## PURPOSE
Replace the current ARC (Adaptive Replacement Cache) buffer cache eviction policy with S3-FIFO, a simpler and often superior eviction algorithm. S3-FIFO uses three FIFO queues — small (admission filter), main, and ghost — with posterior-guided adaptive queue splitting.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 15.1:
- S3-FIFO (Yang et al., 2023): three-queue FIFO eviction with admission filter
- Often matches or beats ARC on filesystem workloads with simpler implementation
- No per-access metadata update (unlike ARC which adjusts T1/T2 split on every access)
- Ghost queue provides scan resistance without LRU overhead

## DESIGN

### Three Queues
1. Small Queue (S): admission filter, ~10% of total cache
   - New blocks enter here
   - Evicted from S on first access = not promoted (scan filter)
   - Evicted from S with re-access = promoted to Main
2. Main Queue (M): ~90% of total cache
   - Blocks promoted from S
   - FIFO eviction (no reordering on access)
   - Frequency counter: blocks with count > 0 get second chance
3. Ghost Queue (G): metadata-only (no data), tracks recently evicted
   - Stores block IDs of recently evicted blocks
   - Hit in ghost = block is popular, immediate promotion to Main on re-admission

### Posterior-Guided Adaptive Split
- Bayesian posterior over optimal S/M split ratio
- Update based on ghost hit rate: high ghost hits = S too small
- This extends vanilla S3-FIFO with principled adaptation (alien artifact methodology)

### Drop-In Replacement
- Same BlockCache trait interface as current ARC
- get(block_id) -> Option<BlockData>
- insert(block_id, data) -> Option<(block_id, data)> // evicted entry
- Configurability: total_capacity, small_ratio (default 0.1)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] S3-FIFO implements BlockCache trait
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for ARC (feature flag to select)
- [ ] Benchmark: hit rate comparison vs ARC on filesystem trace
- [ ] Ghost queue provides scan resistance
- [ ] Adaptive split improves over fixed 10/90 split
- [ ] Unit tests: basic FIFO eviction, promotion, ghost hits

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E cache test (bd-1c4y.3) passes
3. S3-FIFO resists sequential scan pollution
4. Hit rate improvement over plain ARC on mixed workloads
5. No unbounded memory growth in ghost queue","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:17.088023348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:28.174274437Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","eviction","s3fifo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y","depends_on_id":"bd-3rix","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:42.264128830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":100,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.

- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1c4y.1","title":"Implement S3-FIFO eviction for ffs-block ARC cache","description":"# Implement S3-FIFO eviction for ffs-block ARC cache

## GOAL
Implement the S3-FIFO eviction algorithm as a drop-in replacement for the current ARC cache in the ffs-block crate.

## DESIGN

### Data Structures
```rust
struct S3FifoCache<K, V> {
    small: VecDeque<(K, V, u8)>,      // (key, value, access_count)
    main: VecDeque<(K, V, u8)>,       // (key, value, access_count)
    ghost: VecDeque<K>,                // key only, no data
    index: HashMap<K, QueueLocation>, // O(1) lookup
    small_capacity: usize,            // ~10% of total
    main_capacity: usize,             // ~90% of total
    ghost_capacity: usize,            // same as total
}
```

### Operations
- get(key): lookup in index, increment access_count, return data
- insert(key, value):
  1. If key in ghost: insert directly to Main (popular block returning)
  2. Else: insert to Small
  3. If Small full: evict from Small
     - If evicted had access_count > 0: promote to Main
     - Else: move to Ghost, discard data
  4. If Main full: evict from Main
     - If evicted had access_count > 0: re-insert at tail (second chance), decrement count
     - Else: discard

### Integration
- Feature flag: --features s3fifo to select S3-FIFO over ARC
- Same BlockCache trait interface
- Location: ffs-block/src/cache/s3fifo.rs

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Implements BlockCache trait identically to ARC
- [ ] Small queue filters one-hit-wonder scans
- [ ] Main queue provides frequency-based retention
- [ ] Ghost queue enables fast re-admission of popular evicted blocks
- [ ] Feature flag selects S3-FIFO vs ARC at compile time
- [ ] Unit tests: insert, get, eviction order, ghost hit promotion

 {"id":"bd-1c4y.2","title":"Benchmark: S3-FIFO vs ARC hit rates on filesystem workloads","description":"# Benchmark: S3-FIFO vs ARC hit rates on filesystem workloads

## GOAL
Compare S3-FIFO and ARC cache hit rates on representative filesystem workloads to validate the replacement.

## WORKLOADS
1. Sequential scan: read 10000 blocks sequentially (S3-FIFO should excel — scan resistant)
2. Zipf distribution: random block access with Zipf popularity (both should perform well)
3. Mixed: 70% sequential scan + 30% random hot-key access
4. Compile workload: simulate gcc-like access pattern (many small files, some large)
5. Database workload: B-tree traversal pattern (root hot, leaves cold)

## METRICS
- Hit rate (%)
- Operations per second
- Memory overhead per cached block
- p50/p95/p99 lookup latency

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] S3-FIFO matches or beats ARC on >= 3 of 5 workloads
- [ ] Memory overhead per block is <= ARC
- [ ] Results documented with reproducible benchmark harness
- [ ] Baseline captured for regression tracking","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:39.580753067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:27.797909896Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","cache"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:39.580753067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-32yn.2","title":"Fault injection: partial repair symbols (incomplete RaptorQ)","description":"# Fault injection: partial repair symbols (incomplete RaptorQ)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior when RaptorQ repair symbols are partially available — some symbols corrupted, some missing, or insufficient symbols for full recovery.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Some repair symbols corrupted
- Corrupt 20% of repair symbols in a block group
- Inject data corruption within RaptorQ decode capacity
- Expected: repair succeeds using remaining symbols

### Scenario 2: Repair symbols insufficient
- Corrupt enough repair symbols that decode fails
- Inject data corruption that now cannot be repaired
- Expected: graceful failure, evidence record logged, no silent data loss

### Scenario 3: Symbol metadata corrupted
- Corrupt the symbol header (checksums, group descriptors)
- Expected: symbols detected as invalid, repair falls back to re-encode or fails gracefully

### Scenario 4: Stale symbols (data changed after symbol generation)
- Write new data to blocks, do NOT refresh repair symbols
- Corrupt the new data blocks
- Expected: repair restores OLD data (stale), system detects version mismatch via BLAKE3

### Scenario 5: Symbol storage I/O errors
- Inject I/O errors when reading repair symbol blocks
- Expected: repair fails gracefully, evidence logged, no panic

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Partial symbol corruption: repair succeeds when capacity allows
- [ ] Insufficient symbols: graceful failure with evidence
- [ ] Stale symbols: version mismatch detected
- [ ] I/O errors: no panic, graceful degradation
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic and CI-executable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:11.847203864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T03:53:53.964225759Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T03:53:53.964205691Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: tests for partial symbol corruption, insufficient symbols, stale symbols, metadata corruption, and symbol I/O errors exist in ffs-repair","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fault","raptorq","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.2","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:43.462398626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.2","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:11.847203864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":128,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-32yn.3","title":"Fault injection: metadata corruption (superblock, GDT, inodes)","description":"# Fault injection: metadata corruption (superblock, GDT, inodes)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior when critical metadata structures are corrupted. Metadata corruption is catastrophic — it can make the entire filesystem unreadable.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Superblock corruption
- Corrupt primary superblock fields (magic number, block count, inode count)
- Expected: mount fails with clear error, backup superblock used if available

### Scenario 2: Group Descriptor Table corruption
- Corrupt GDT entries (block bitmap location, inode bitmap, inode table)
- Expected: affected block groups inaccessible, other groups still readable

### Scenario 3: Inode corruption
- Corrupt individual inode fields (size, block count, extent tree root)
- Expected: affected file inaccessible, other files intact

### Scenario 4: Inode bitmap corruption
- Corrupt inode allocation bitmap
- Expected: allocation inconsistency detected, defensive read behavior

### Scenario 5: Block bitmap corruption
- Corrupt block allocation bitmap
- Expected: allocation inconsistency detected, read-only degradation

### Scenario 6: Extent tree internal node corruption
- Corrupt B-tree internal node in extent tree
- Expected: file partially readable (extents before corruption accessible)

### Recovery Testing
- For each scenario: verify RaptorQ repair (if metadata has 2x overhead) can recover
- If repair impossible: verify graceful degradation and clear error reporting

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Each metadata corruption type produces appropriate error response
- [ ] No panics from corrupted metadata
- [ ] Checksums detect corruption before use (no silent data misinterpretation)
- [ ] RaptorQ metadata repair works for recoverable cases
- [ ] Evidence ledger records all detected corruption
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic and CI-executable","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:24.051971520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T04:10:22.007028484Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corruption","fault","metadata"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:24.252718351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:24.051971520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":127,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-32yn.4","title":"Fault injection: I/O throttling (slow I/O, deadline misses)","description":"# Fault injection: I/O throttling (slow I/O, deadline misses)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior under degraded I/O performance — simulating slow disks, network storage latency, or I/O congestion.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Uniform slow I/O
- Add 50ms latency to every read/write operation
- Expected: filesystem remains functional, FUSE operations complete (slowly)
- Measure: FUSE operation timeout handling, queue depths

### Scenario 2: Intermittent I/O stalls
- Random 500ms-2s stalls on 5% of I/O operations
- Expected: no deadlocks, backpressure activates, operations eventually complete

### Scenario 3: I/O bandwidth limit
- Limit to 10MB/s read, 5MB/s write (simulating NAS or USB)
- Expected: FlushDaemon adapts, no OOM from dirty block accumulation

### Scenario 4: I/O timeout
- I/O operations that take > 30s return ETIMEDOUT
- Expected: operation fails gracefully, no resource leak

### Scenario 5: Progressive degradation
- Start with normal I/O, gradually increase latency over 60s
- Expected: backpressure system activates, evidence ledger records degradation

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Pluggable I/O layer with configurable delay, bandwidth limit, timeout
- Delay injection: uniform, gaussian, or bimodal distribution
- Integration: inject at ffs-block I/O boundary

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] No deadlocks under any I/O degradation scenario
- [ ] Backpressure correctly activates under I/O stalls
- [ ] No OOM from unbounded dirty block accumulation during slow I/O
- [ ] Operations fail gracefully on timeout (no resource leak)
- [ ] All scenarios CI-executable with deterministic delays","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:36.214496847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26.362143810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fault","io","throttle"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.4","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:36.214496847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":168,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

-{"id":"bd-32yn.5","title":"Crash-replay test suite: 500 random schedules with injected crashes","description":"# Crash-replay test suite: 500 random schedules with injected crashes

## GOAL
Run 500 deterministic crash-replay scenarios: each generates a random sequence of filesystem operations, injects a crash at a random point, recovers, and verifies consistency.

## DESIGN

### Operation Generator
- Random sequence of: create, write, read, rename, unlink, mkdir, rmdir, fsync
- Operations parameterized: file size, directory depth, name length
- Deterministic: seeded PRNG produces reproducible sequences
- Length: 100-1000 operations per schedule

### Crash Point Selection
- For each schedule, select 1-3 random crash points
- Crash point = operation index + I/O sub-operation (e.g., crash during 3rd block write of operation #47)
- Ensures coverage of: mid-write, mid-fsync, mid-rename, mid-unlink crashes

### Recovery and Verification
After each crash:
1. Mount filesystem (triggers crash recovery)
2. Full filesystem walk: verify all accessible files are readable
3. Consistency check: no dangling inodes, no double-allocated blocks
4. Committed data check: all fsync'd data intact
5. Evidence ledger check: recovery actions logged

### Reporting
- Per-schedule: pass/fail, crash point, recovery time, evidence summary
- Aggregate: pass rate, mean recovery time, most common failure mode
- Any failure: save full reproducer (seed, schedule, crash point)

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Test harness: Rust test binary with configurable seed range
- Parallelizable: each schedule is independent (run on multiple cores)
- CI: run all 500 schedules on every PR (target: < 10 minutes total)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] 500 schedules generated and executed
- [ ] 100% pass rate (zero consistency violations after recovery)
- [ ] All fsync'd data preserved after crash
- [ ] Failure reproducers saved for any failing schedule
- [ ] Total runtime < 10 minutes on CI hardware
- [ ] Every schedule deterministic (same seed = same result)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:52.676947222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19.482390862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","deterministic","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:22.037666789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:52.676947222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":126,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B

Fallback trigger: static config profile

-{"id":"bd-32yn.6","title":"Conformance: fsx-style stress test with corruption injection","description":"# Conformance: fsx-style stress test with corruption injection

## GOAL
Implement an fsx-style (File System eXerciser) stress test that performs random file operations while continuously verifying data integrity. Extended with FrankenFS-specific corruption injection to test self-healing under stress.

## BACKGROUND
fsx (from LTP/xfstests) is the gold standard for filesystem stress testing. It performs random reads, writes, truncates, and mmaps on a file, maintaining an in-memory reference copy. After each operation, it verifies the file matches the reference. Any mismatch is a bug.

## DESIGN

### Operations (weighted random selection)
- write(offset, length, data): 40%
- read(offset, length) + verify: 25%
- truncate(length): 10%
- fsync(): 10%
- fallocate(offset, length): 5%
- punch_hole(offset, length): 5%
- close + reopen: 5%

### Verification
- In-memory reference buffer tracks expected file content
- After each read: compare against reference
- Periodically: close file, reopen, read entire file, compare against reference
- After unmount + remount: verify entire file against reference

### Corruption Injection Extension
- Every N operations (configurable, default 1000): inject random block corruption
- Wait for scrub daemon to detect and repair
- Verify file still matches reference after repair
- Evidence ledger check: repair logged correctly

### Parameters
- File size: configurable (default 64MB)
- Operation count: configurable (default 100000)
- Seed: deterministic
- Duration mode: run for N seconds instead of N operations

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Zero data mismatches across 100K operations
- [ ] Survives corruption injection + repair cycles
- [ ] Deterministic with seed (reproducible failures)
- [ ] Runs in < 5 minutes for default parameters
- [ ] Clear failure report with exact operation that triggered mismatch","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:07.106145900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19.325863536Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fsx","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.6","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.5","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:23.139598637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.6","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:07.106145900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":125,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: quality_assurance
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.1 Optimization Loop + 0.7 Artifact Contract + 6.12 Proptest
EV score: 50 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: bench_runs>=10, sample_floor=30 for noisy paths
Fallback trigger: golden-only gate, disable adaptive controller deployment

- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19Z"}]}

### Verification

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

 {"id":"bd-32yn.7","title":"E2E test: xfstests generic and ext4 subsets","description":"# E2E test: xfstests generic and ext4 subsets

## GOAL
Run the standard Linux filesystem test suite (xfstests/fstests) against FrankenFS FUSE mount to verify compatibility with expected filesystem semantics.

## BACKGROUND
xfstests (github.com/kdave/xfstests) is the authoritative filesystem test suite used by all major Linux filesystems. It contains:
- generic/: tests applicable to all POSIX filesystems (~600 tests)
- ext4/: tests specific to ext4 semantics (~100 tests)

## APPROACH

### Phase 1: Generic Subset
Run xfstests generic/ tests that apply to FUSE filesystems. Exclude tests requiring:
- Direct I/O (O_DIRECT) — FUSE may not support
- Filesystem-specific ioctls
- Quota management
- ACLs (unless we implement them)
- Kernel-specific features (fiemap, etc.)

Expected pass rate: start with identifying which tests pass, create tracking for regressions.

### Phase 2: ext4 Subset
Run xfstests ext4/ tests. Many require mkfs.ext4 features we may not support yet.
Subset: tests covering basic file operations, directory operations, extent handling.

### Infrastructure
- xfstests requires: TEST_DEV (FrankenFS FUSE mount), SCRATCH_DEV (second mount for temp data)
- Wrapper script: configure xfstests for FrankenFS, run selected tests, capture results
- CI integration: run on every PR, track pass/fail count over time

### Known Exclusions
- Tests requiring root (CAP_SYS_ADMIN)
- Tests requiring kernel features not available in FUSE
- Tests for features we explicitly don't implement (quotas, ACLs, etc.)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] xfstests infrastructure configured for FrankenFS
- [ ] Generic test subset identified and documented
- [ ] ext4 test subset identified and documented
- [ ] CI integration: test results tracked per commit
- [ ] Pass rate monotonically increases over time
- [ ] Regression: any previously passing test that fails blocks merge","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:22.445755731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19.178500479Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2e","xfstests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.7","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:22.445755731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":124,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-32yn.8","title":"Add unit tests for fault injection framework harness","description":"# Add unit tests for fault injection framework harness

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Testing and Fault Injection epic (bd-32yn). The epic has many E2E/integration test beads (bd-32yn.1 through bd-32yn.7) but NO unit tests for the fault injection framework itself -- the pluggable I/O layer, failure point registration, deterministic replay, and crash-point scheduling.

## TEST PLAN

### Pluggable I/O Layer Tests
1. FaultInjector wraps BlockDevice transparently (no faults = passthrough)
2. fail_at_byte(offset) triggers fault at exact byte
3. fail_on_read(block) returns injected error
4. fail_on_write(block) returns injected error
5. Fault cleared after trigger (one-shot mode)
6. Persistent fault mode (fault on every access)

### Deterministic Replay Tests
7. Same seed produces same fault sequence
8. Fault sequence is reproducible across runs
9. Fault log captures all injected faults with timestamps

### Crash Point Tests
10. Crash point registered at specific operation
11. Crash point fires at correct moment (before/after fsync)
12. Recovery after crash point: filesystem mounts cleanly

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 12 tests above. Deterministic.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Fault injected (debug): fault_type, target_block, operation
- Crash point fired (info): crash_point_id, operation_in_flight

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 12 tests pass
2. Framework is reusable across all fault injection beads
3. Deterministic replay verified","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:32.985112387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:24.326422449Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["faultinjection","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.8","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:32.985112387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":156,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-375","title":"EPIC: btrfs Read Path & FUSE Mount","description":"# EPIC: btrfs Read Path & FUSE Mount

## PURPOSE
Enable read-only btrfs FUSE mounting, achieving parity with ext4 read-only support. Currently btrfs metadata parsing is at 35% (7/20).

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md, btrfs gaps:
- btrfs transaction parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- btrfs delayed refs parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- btrfs scrub parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- FUSE mount for btrfs: NOT WORKING

However, significant foundation exists:
- Superblock decode: ✅
- B-tree header decode: ✅
- Leaf item metadata decode: ✅
- Internal node parsing: ✅
- sys_chunk mapping: ✅
- Read-only tree walk: ✅
- BtrfsContext in ffs-core: ✅

The remaining gap is wiring tree walking and extent resolution into `FsOps` and then into the FUSE surface.

## CURRENT STATE
- `ffs-ondisk` has comprehensive btrfs parsing
- `ffs-btrfs` provides `walk_tree`
- `ffs-core` has a `BtrfsContext` stub
- `ffs-fuse` operations currently route to ext4-only `FsOps`

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Implement `BtrfsFsOps` (like `Ext4FsOps` but for btrfs)
2. Wire `ffs-cli mount` path to btrfs ops
3. Implement btrfs inode -> extent resolution sufficient for read-only file reads
4. Add E2E coverage for btrfs mount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount btrfs.img /mnt` works read-only
2. Basic file operations work: `ls`, `cat`, `stat`
3. Subvolume visibility (at least default subvol)
4. Performance baseline established (hyperfine) for inspect + mount smoke
5. btrfs metadata parsing reaches 60%+ parity

## DEPENDENCIES
- EPIC: Conformance & Quality Infrastructure (bd-2jk) - need fixtures/goldens
- btrfs golden fixtures (bd-2jk.2)

## EXPLICIT V1 EXCLUSIONS (per PLAN_TO_PORT_FRANKENFS_TO_RUST.md)
- Multi-device / RAID: OUT OF SCOPE
- Compression: OUT OF SCOPE
- Send/receive: OUT OF SCOPE
- Write support: OUT OF SCOPE

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md §4-6 (btrfs behavior)
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §2 (btrfs format)

## Success Criteria
1. `ffs inspect <btrfs.img>` and `ffs mount <btrfs.img>` work on the golden fixtures without panics.
2. `scripts/e2e/ffs_btrfs_ro_smoke.sh` passes (or skips cleanly when FUSE unavailable) and produces actionable logs.
3. Unit tests cover:
   - inode lookup by key
   - directory enumeration ordering/offsets
   - extent resolution for file reads","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:56:58.365480057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:22.338387217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","fuse"],"comments":[{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-375","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
